title: Colgate-Palmolive Webcasts Presentation at the Barclays 2023 Global Consumer Staples Conference
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) Chairman, President and CEO, Noel Wallace, will present on Wednesday, September 6, 2023 at 7:30 a.m. ET at the Barclays 2023 Global Consumer Staples Conference. Investors may access a live webcast of this presentation and the presentation slides on Colgate's website at http://www.colgatepalmolive.com. For those unable to participate during the live webcast, a recorded version of the webcast will be made available through the Investor Center.

title: Can Its Growth Efforts Aid Colgate (CL) Amid Cost Woes?
text: Despite rising raw material costs and adverse currency, Colgate's (CL) robust innovation, pricing efforts and expansion plans are likely to drive growth in the long term.

title: Colgate-Palmolive: A Healthy 7% Return With A Safe Dividend For Investors In A Post-Pandemic World
text: Colgate-Palmolive's ability to maintain or increase selling prices as needed is a testament to its strong brand and pricing power. Colgate-Palmolive has a strong global presence, with subsidiaries in numerous countries, indicating a diversified risk profile. Colgate-Palmolive has a robust dividend history, with a forward annual dividend yield of 2.53% and a payout ratio of 64.19%.

title: 7 Blue-Chip Stocks Smart Investors Should Be Loading Up on Now
text: There's a reasonable argument to be made that investors should load up on blue-chip stocks regularly. After all, large and well-established firms with long track records of success are likely to continue to be successful.

title: 7 Dividend Stocks to Buy in July 2022
text: Dividend stocks are only becoming more attractive as income becomes increasingly paramount. These seven equities pair dividends with safety.

title: Colgate And The Importance Of Sales Growth, Expectations Matter
text: Colgate recently presented their Q2 2023 report, which first crashed the stock, but during the last week it has rebounded. My main takeaway from the report is that CL continues to achieve organic sales growth. With very conservative expectations build into its current stock price, an options play might be the right way to approach this stock.

title: 4 Soaps & Cleaning Materials Stocks to Gain as Industry Catches On
text: The soap and cleaning materials industry benefits from revived demand trends, pricing actions, innovation, digital transformation and brand building. Players like UL, CHD, PG and CL look well-poised.

title: Buy 5 August Dividend Kings, Watch 4 More
text: "There is only one-criterion to be included among the Dividend Kings: a publicly-traded company must increase its total fiscal-year dividend-payout for a minimum of 50 consecutive-years."--Dogs of the Dow. The 50 Dividend Kings screened as of July 28, 2023, represented nine of eleven Morningstar Sectors. Broker targeted-top-ten net-gainers ranged 13.68%-34.52% topped-by Target, and Gorman-Rupp. By yield, Altria tops-all. Top-ten Kingly August yields from NFG, ABBV, BKH, FRT, NWN, MMM, CDUAF, LEG, UVV, & MO averaged 5.24%.
